100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 10, 1926, the United States Senate was poised to vote on the controversial McNary-Haugen Bill, aimed at providing price supports for struggling farmers. In Europe, political tensions escalated as the German government faced mounting pressure from the Allied powers regarding reparations. Meanwhile, in the world of aviation, excitement grew as Charles Lindbergh announced plans for a transatlantic flight, promising to push the boundaries of modern aviation.
Updated: Friday, April 10, 2026 at 07:37:37 AM CDT
